title: Magnesium Dosage Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Daily Magnesium Dosage by Age and Gender
description: Calculate your daily magnesium dosage by age and gender. Updated 2026 guidelines for optimal health and deficiency prevention.
intro: Reference values in health come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO). They give you a framework to interpret your results, but your personal context is what matters. Daily magnesium dosage by age and gender. Values are updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you current.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **Magnesium RDA**. Direct application with your input values. Daily magnesium dosage by age and gender.
useCases: Technical calculations related to magnesium dosage
explanation:
**Normal reference values in health** come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO, CDC). They vary by age, sex, ethnicity, and region. They're guidelines; your personal medical history always takes precedence.

## How it's calculated

Daily magnesium dosage based on age and gender.

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting you when values fall outside normal parameters.

## Normal reference values

| Parameter | Normal Adult Range |
|---|---|
| Blood Pressure | <120/80 mmHg |
| Resting Heart Rate | 60-100 bpm |
| Body Temperature | 36.1-37.2°C |
| SpO2 | 95-100% |
| Fasting Glucose | 70-100 mg/dL |
| HbA1c | <5.7% |
| Total Cholesterol | <200 mg/dL |
| LDL | <100 mg/dL |
| HDL | >40 (M) / >50 (F) |

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guideline. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a specialized professional. Values are updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How much magnesium should I take daily?
faq.a: Men: 400-420 mg. Women: 310-320 mg. Pregnant women: 350-400 mg.

faq.q: What are the signs of magnesium deficiency?
faq.a: Muscle cramps, fatigue, insomnia, heart palpitations, migraines.
Poor diet, alcohol use, diarrhea, diuretics, chronic stress.

faq.q: What types of magnesium supplements are available?
faq.a: Glycinate (high absorption), citrate (gentle laxative), oxide (cheap, lower bioavailability).
If your diet is insufficient or you have symptoms. Typical dose: 200-400 mg/day.

faq.q: What is the maximum safe magnesium dose?
faq.a: 350 mg from supplements (dietary sources have no limit).
It reduces absorption of antibiotics and thyroid medications. Separate intake by 2-4 hours.

faq.q: When should I see a doctor about magnesium?
faq.a: If values are outside normal ranges, if you have related symptoms, or if you have a family history of cardiovascular or metabolic diseases.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data update needed
